factors.summary: The company has issued senior unsecured promissory notes, indicating a moderate approach to debt management.

evidence.text: Net cash used in financing activities in the first nine months of 2017 consisted of the payment of dividends, purchases of our common stock, and payments against debt obligations, which were partially offset by proceeds from the exercise of stock options and proceeds from debt obligations, including the issuance of a new series of senior unsecured promissory notes under our master note agreement in the aggregate principal amount of $60.0. The notes bear interest at a fixed rate of 3.22% per annum and are due and payable in full on March 1, 2024, and were issued to fund the purchase price of the Mansco acquisition.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, supported by increases in customer activity and market metrics.

evidence.text: The increases in net sales in the periods noted above for 2017 and 2016, were driven primarily by higher unit sales. Price was not a material factor in the third quarter and first nine months of 2017 compared to the same periods of 2016. The higher unit sales resulted primarily from two sources. The first is improvement in underlying market demand. We believe the improvement in general business activity is reflected in a number of metrics. For instance, the Purchasing Managers Index...averaged 57.0, 55.8, and 58.6 in the first, second, and third quarters of 2017, respectively, well above 49.8, 51.5, and 51.2 in the first, second, and third quarters of 2016, respectively.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards non-fastener products and larger customers, which are contributing to gross profit margin compression.

evidence.text: Branches typically achieve higher average net sales disproportionately by growth in the non-fastener product lines and with large customers, causing gross profit margin to decline as average net sales grow. From the first nine months of 2016 to the first nine months of 2017, our daily sales of fastener products grew 6.9% (of which 2.5 percentage points were attributable to Mansco) while our daily sales of non-fastener products grew 12.1%. The relatively slower growth that we experienced in the first nine months of 2017 in our fastener product line combined with relatively faster growth in sales to our largest customers partially offset the gross profit improvement we otherwise experienced in the period.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by higher unit sales and market demand.

evidence.text: The increases in net sales in the periods noted above for 2017 and 2016, were driven primarily by higher unit sales. Price was not a material factor in the third quarter and first nine months of 2017 compared to the same periods of 2016. The higher unit sales resulted primarily from two sources. The first is improvement in underlying market demand. We believe the improvement in general business activity is reflected in a number of metrics. For instance, the Purchasing Managers Index...averaged 57.0, 55.8, and 58.6 in the first, second, and third quarters of 2017, respectively, well above 49.8, 51.5, and 51.2 in the first, second, and third quarters of 2016, respectively.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by real demand rather than price increases.

evidence.text: The increases in net sales in the periods noted above for 2017 and 2016, were driven primarily by higher unit sales. Price was not a material factor in the third quarter and first nine months of 2017 compared to the same periods of 2016. The higher unit sales resulted primarily from two sources. The first is improvement in underlying market demand.

factors.summary: The manufacturing and non-residential construction markets are growing fastest, supported by market metrics and customer growth.

evidence.text: The manufacturing market includes both original equipment manufacturers (OEM) and maintenance, repair, and operations (MRO). The non-residential construction market includes general, electrical, plumbing, sheet metal, and road contractors. Other users of our products include farmers, truckers, railroads, oil exploration, production, and refinement companies, mining companies, federal, state, and local governmental entities, schools, and certain retail trades. Geographically, our branches and customers are primarily located in North America. Our motto is Growth through Customer Service. We are a growth-centric organization focused on identifying 'drivers' that will allow us to get closer to our customers and gain market share in what we believe remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to mix changes and other factors, though there are signs of improvement.

evidence.text: Our gross profit percentage for the first nine months of 2017 benefited from accelerating revenue growth rates, progress in our supply chain initiatives, as well as increased discipline in purchasing throughout the organization. This was offset by a roughly 30 basis point drag owing to two elements of mix. The first was a change in product and customer mix. Fasteners, which is currently our largest single product line at approximately 35.8% of sales, is our highest gross profit product line due to the high transaction cost surrounding the sourcing and supply of the product for customers. Any reduction in the mix of our sales attributable to fasteners, and particularly maintenance fasteners, may negatively impact gross profit. Larger customers (for which National Accounts are a good proxy), whose more focused buying patterns allow us to offer them better pricing, also influence gross profit. Branches typically achieve higher average net sales disproportionately by growth in the non-fastener product lines and with large customers, causing gross profit margin to decline as average net sales grow.

factors.summary: Productivity initiatives are contributing to margin improvements, though challenges remain.

evidence.text: Our gross profit percentage for the first nine months of 2017 benefited from accelerating revenue growth rates, progress in our supply chain initiatives, as well as increased discipline in purchasing throughout the organization. This was offset by a roughly 30 basis point drag owing to two elements of mix.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is improving, contributing to margin improvements.

evidence.text: Our gross profit percentage for the first nine months of 2017 benefited from accelerating revenue growth rates, progress in our supply chain initiatives, as well as increased discipline in purchasing throughout the organization.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are growing, driven by increased demand and the Mansco acquisition.

evidence.text: The most significant contributors to the increase in inventory from September 30, 2016 to September 30, 2017 were the completion of the CSP 16 rollout in 2016, increased demand, and the Mansco acquisition in 2017.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share, particularly through growth initiatives like Onsite locations and national accounts.

evidence.text: During the third quarter of 2017, we signed 42 new national account contracts (defined as new customer accounts with a multi-site contract). Additionally, we signed 81 new Onsite customer locations (defined as dedicated sales and service provided from within, or in close proximity to, the customer's facility) and 4,771 new industrial vending machines in the third quarter of 2017.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is a factor, with larger customers influencing gross profit margins.

evidence.text: Larger customers (for which National Accounts are a good proxy), whose more focused buying patterns allow us to offer them better pricing, also influence gross profit. Branches typically achieve higher average net sales disproportionately by growth in the non-fastener product lines and with large customers, causing gross profit margin to decline as average net sales grow.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to decrease, signaling cautiousness about future growth.

evidence.text: Cash requirements for capital expenditures were satisfied from cash generated from operations, available cash and cash equivalents, our borrowing capacity, and the proceeds of disposals. Our 2017 net capital expenditures spend expectation remains at approximately $127.0.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Investing Activities

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, supported by net earnings growth.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities increased in the first nine months of 2017 relative to the first nine months of 2016, primarily due to our net earnings growth.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

factors.summary: Mergers and acquisitions activity includes the Mansco acquisition, which has had a mixed impact on margins.

evidence.text: The second element of mix was driven by the acquisition of Mansco. Mansco products carry a lower gross profit product mix than the Company.

factors.summary: The company is in growth mode, increasing headcount to support customer growth and business initiatives.

evidence.text: During the last twelve months, we have increased our headcount by 201 people in our in-market units and 378 people in total. The remaining increase is mostly a function of additions we have made to support customer growth in the field as well as investments in our growth drivers.

factors.summary: The economic environment is a tailwind, with improving economic conditions contributing to business growth.

evidence.text: The manufacturing market includes both original equipment manufacturers (OEM) and maintenance, repair, and operations (MRO). The non-residential construction market includes general, electrical, plumbing, sheet metal, and road contractors. Other users of our products include farmers, truckers, railroads, oil exploration, production, and refinement companies, mining companies, federal, state, and local governmental entities, schools, and certain retail trades. Geographically, our branches and customers are primarily located in North America. Our motto is Growth through Customer Service. We are a growth-centric organization focused on identifying 'drivers' that will allow us to get closer to our customers and gain market share in what we believe remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: Higher interest rates are increasing net interest expense, putting pressure on financial performance.

evidence.text: Our net interest expense was $6.2 in the first nine months of 2017 and $2.5 in the third quarter of 2017, compared to $4.4 in the first nine months of 2016 and $1.7 in the third quarter of 2016. These increases were mainly caused by higher average interest rates over both periods.

factors.summary: The company is actively repurchasing shares, indicating confidence in its stock's value, with evidence of significant buybacks in recent years. However, there is no explicit mention of shareholder returns in the Liquidity and Capital Resources subsection, which may suggest a lack of focus on returning value through dividends or other means.

evidence.text: During the first nine months of 2017, we purchased 1,900,000 shares of our common stock at an average price of approximately $43.43 per share. During the first nine months of 2016, we purchased 1,600,000 shares of our common stock at an average price of approximately $37.15 per share. On July 11, 2017, our board of directors authorized purchases by us of 5,000,000 shares of our common stock. We currently have authority to purchase up to 4,400,000 shares under this authorization.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

evidence.text: No specific information on buybacks or dividends was found in the text.
